meta.changelog: Initial content layer. Framework guidance validated against: ITIL 4 current edition (AXELOS/PeopleCert), DORA dora.dev 2024 State of DevOps, SAFe 6.0, EU DORA Regulation 2022/2554 (in force Jan 2025), FCA PS21/3 Operational Resilience, UK GDPR/ICO guidance. Review quarterly or when AXELOS, DORA, FCA or EBA publish material updates.
meta.maintainer: Increment content_version to YYYY-MM-DD when updating. This file is fetched on page load to check for newer content than the baked-in version in the HTML.

content_warnings.title: EU DORA in force from January 2025
content_warnings.text: ICT change management obligations under DORA Article 9 apply to in-scope EU financial entities. Change management processes — including release management gates and approvals — must meet the requirements of Articles 9 and 10. Verify your firm's obligations with your compliance team.

content_warnings.title: ITIL 4 (current edition) — verify against axelos.com
content_warnings.text: Framework guidance reflects ITIL 4 current edition (AXELOS/PeopleCert). Practice definitions are held in this content layer — not compiled into the app — so guidance can update without requiring app changes. As ITIL evolves, updated definitions and version-aware notes will be added here. For organisations running hybrid ITIL environments, adopt at the pace that suits your organisation. Verify at axelos.com for the latest edition status.

content_warnings.title: EBA — EU DORA and operational resilience — new publication detected
content_warnings.text: EU/EEA banks remain resilient amid rising geopolitical, market and technological risks. Review for relevance to your EU DORA obligations. Always verify at the authoritative source.
content_warnings.authorityName: EU DORA — Digital Operational Resilience Act (EUR-Lex Official Journal)

dora_article_9.title: DORA Article 9 — ICT security policies, procedures, protocols and tools
dora_article_9.summary: Financial entities must implement ICT change management procedures. All changes to ICT systems must be subject to formal approval by authorised personnel prior to implementation, with adequate testing and post-implementation review.

fca_ps21_3.title: FCA PS21/3 — Building Operational Resilience
fca_ps21_3.summary: FCA-regulated firms must identify important business services, set impact tolerances, and ensure their change programmes do not breach those tolerances. Major releases affecting important business services require robust testing and rollback plans.

uk_gdpr_article_33.title: UK GDPR Article 33 — Personal data breach notification
uk_gdpr_article_33.summary: Personal data breaches must be notified to the ICO within 72 hours of becoming aware. Releases that cause data breaches trigger this obligation. Controller must document all breaches.

deployment_frequency.high: Between once per day and once per week
deployment_frequency.medium: Between once per week and once per month

lead_time_for_changes.high: Between one day and one week
lead_time_for_changes.medium: Between one week and one month
lead_time_for_changes.low: Between one month and six months

failed_deployment_recovery_time.medium: Between one day and one week
failed_deployment_recovery_time.low: Between one week and one month

ai_rule_overrides._comment: Override AI hint text and levels without editing the HTML. Match the 'id' field on RELEASE_AI_RULES and DEFECT_AI_RULES. Update when regulatory guidance changes.

payment.hint: Updated hint text referencing new regulatory guidance...

framework_updates.update: Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 in force from 17 January 2025. Article 9 change management obligations apply to all in-scope financial entities. ICT change management procedures must include formal approval by authorised personnel prior to implementation.
ITIL 4 Release Management practice guide current edition. AXELOS publish periodic updates — verify at axelos.com for any amendments since this content version.
DORA 2024 State of DevOps Report published. Key finding: elite performers deploy on-demand with CFR below 5% and MTTR under 1 hour. See dora.dev for updated performance bands.
SAFe 6.0 is the current edition. SAFe 5.x guidance remains broadly applicable. Verify framework version with your SAFe Practice Consultant.

platform_notes.jira_note: Jira REST API v3 field names current as of 2026. Project-specific field IDs (customfield_xxxxx) vary by instance.
platform_notes.ado_note: Azure DevOps REST API 7.1 current as of 2026. Work item type and field names vary by process template (Agile, Scrum, CMMI).
platform_notes.servicenow_note: ServiceNow change management table: change_request. Key fields: short_description, description, risk, impact, assignment_group, cmdb_ci, planned_start_date, planned_end_date.
